summary: Clinicians assessing febrile patients with sickle cell disease rely heavily on symptom-based diagnoses like fever and cough, which are non-specific and commonly seen in infections such as bacterial pneumonia. A definitive diagnosis requires new lung infiltrates visible on chest X-ray; without this imaging criterion, clinical judgment lacks accuracy due to overlapping presentations among various diseases.
